title: mldsa_sign: no guard against sig_len > cap before truncate
description: wolfhsm_mldsa_sign is passed sig_len=cap (the pre-allocated buffer size) as an IN/OUT parameter. After the call, the code does sig.truncate(sig_len as usize) with no check that sig_len <= cap. If a misbehaving server writes back sig_len > cap, truncate is a no-op and the returned Vec has length cap — silently misrepresenting the actual signature length. Every other operation in the crate (ECC sign, RSA, ECDH, AES-GCM) has an explicit 'if out_len > buf.capacity() { return Err(ProtocolError) }' guard before set_len/truncate. ML-DSA is the only one that skips this check.
